questions.question: Explain why a real image formed by a concave mirror can be captured on a screen.
questions.answer: A real image is formed where reflected rays actually converge, so light from the image falls on the screen, producing a visible image.

questions.question: Why do shadows have sharp edges when formed by a point light source?
questions.answer: Because all light rays come from a single point, the shadow is sharply defined with no overlap of rays.

questions.question: Describe why a convex mirror image is always diminished and virtual.
questions.answer: Convex mirrors diverge rays, so the reflected rays never meet; the brain extends them backward to form a smaller virtual image.

questions.question: Explain the role of the gnomon in a sundial.
questions.answer: The gnomon casts a shadow on the dial, which moves as the Sun changes position, indicating the time.

questions.question: Why is the penumbra formed around the umbra?
questions.answer: Because some light from the extended light source reaches the edges of the object, partially illuminating the surrounding area.

questions.question: Why is lateral inversion observed in images of plane mirrors?
questions.answer: Plane mirrors reverse the left and right sides of the image while keeping the front and back orientation unchanged.

questions.question: Explain why a concave mirror forms a virtual image when the object is within the focal length.
questions.answer: The reflected rays diverge, and the brain traces them backward, forming a magnified virtual image behind the mirror.

questions.question: How does diffused reflection make objects visible from different angles?
questions.answer: Diffused reflection scatters light in many directions, so eyes at different positions receive light, allowing visibility from multiple angles.

questions.question: Why does the length of a shadow change throughout the day?
questions.answer: Because the Sun’s position changes, altering the angle of incident light and thus the shadow length.

questions.question: Explain why images formed by convex mirrors cannot be projected on a screen.
questions.answer: Because the reflected rays diverge and never actually meet, forming a virtual image that exists only in perception.

questions.question: Why are shadows fuzzy when formed by an extended light source?
questions.answer: Different points of the extended source produce overlapping light rays, causing a gradual transition between light and dark regions.

questions.question: Why is diffused reflection important for reading books?
questions.answer: It scatters light uniformly from the paper so the letters can be seen clearly without glare.

questions.question: Explain the effect of moving an object closer to a light source on shadow size.
questions.answer: The shadow becomes larger because the light rays diverge more as they pass the closer object.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror produce an inverted real image for distant objects?
questions.answer: When the object is beyond the focal point, reflected rays converge in front of the mirror, forming a real inverted image.

questions.question: How do periscopes use plane mirrors to let a person see over obstacles?
questions.answer: By reflecting light from one mirror at the top to another at the bottom, directing the image to the viewer’s eye.

questions.question: Why does a convex mirror reduce the size of the image?
questions.answer: Because it diverges reflected rays, making the brain perceive a smaller image.

questions.question: Why are shadows longest in the morning and evening?
questions.answer: Because the Sun is low on the horizon, producing shallow angles of incidence for light.

questions.question: Explain why lateral inversion occurs in plane mirrors but not in concave mirrors.
questions.answer: Plane mirrors reverse left and right; concave mirrors form real or virtual images without lateral inversion depending on object distance.

questions.question: What happens to shadow size when the object moves away from the light source?
questions.answer: The shadow becomes smaller because the rays converge more around the distant object.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror produce a magnified image when the object is very close?
questions.answer: Because the object is within the focal length, causing reflected rays to diverge and appear larger.

questions.question: Why do convex mirrors provide a wider field of view?
questions.answer: Because they diverge rays, allowing the reflected image to cover a larger area.

questions.question: How does the Sun’s position affect the direction of shadows?
questions.answer: Shadows change direction as the Sun moves across the sky, altering the angle of incident light.

questions.question: Why is a real image inverted while a virtual image is upright?
questions.answer: A real image is formed by converging rays in front of a mirror, flipping it; a virtual image is formed by diverging rays, appearing upright.

questions.question: Explain why convex mirrors are used in traffic safety mirrors.
questions.answer: They provide a diminished, upright image with a wide field of view, helping drivers see a larger area.

questions.question: Why do some light sources produce fuzzy shadows while others produce sharp shadows?
questions.answer: Point sources produce sharp shadows, while extended sources produce fuzzy shadows due to overlapping light rays.
